key_insight: High oscillating laser frequency can unexpectedly increase weld penetration depth because it alters melt-flow behavior rather than simply spreading the heat input.
engineering_relevance: The result helps optimize oscillating laser welding for deeper, more stable joints by tuning frequency to control molten-pool dynamics.
method: Analyzed oscillating laser welding behavior through melt-flow mechanisms.
Linked high-frequency oscillation to changes in penetration depth.
Interpreted the anomaly using process-physics observations from the welding melt pool.
evidence_signal: The abstract indicates a journal article focused on mechanistic analysis, but it does not report specific numerical datasets or evaluation metrics.
watch_item: The effect may depend strongly on material system, beam settings, and weld geometry, so the frequency-penetration relationship may not generalize without process-specific calibration.
paper_title: Mechanism of anomalous increase in penetration depth at high oscillating frequency in oscillating laser welding based on melt flow
